steps.title: Separate two clauses
steps.content: The sentence contrasts two independent clauses: an Elvis taxon is not the real thing, and it is a misidentified look-alike. A comma alone cannot join those clauses.

steps.title: Reject comma splices
steps.content: Choice A redundantly combines however with but and still lacks proper clause separation. Choice B creates a comma splice. Choice C also creates a comma splice by using only commas around however.

steps.title: Choose the sentence break
steps.content: The remaining version lets however modify the first clause and then uses a period before It to begin the second independent clause. The answer is D.
